title: Technology Basics
description: Question batch for the Internet and Networking Basics domain.

questions.question: Which statement best describes IP address?
answers: A numeric label used to identify a device on a network.
A device that connects a home or office network to an internet provider.
A service that translates domain names into IP addresses.
The web address used to locate a page or resource online.
questions.explanation: IP address is a numeric label used to identify a device on a network.

questions.question: What does Router refer to?
answers: A device that directs network traffic between devices and networks.
A service that translates domain names into IP addresses.
The web address used to locate a page or resource online.
A separate page open within the same browser window.
questions.explanation: Router is a device that directs network traffic between devices and networks.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Modem?
answers: A device that connects a home or office network to an internet provider.
The web address used to locate a page or resource online.
A separate page open within the same browser window.
The transfer of data from the internet to a local device.
questions.explanation: Modem is a device that connects a home or office network to an internet provider.

questions.question: Which statement best describes DNS?
answers: A service that translates domain names into IP addresses.
A separate page open within the same browser window.
The transfer of data from the internet to a local device.
The transfer of data from a local device to the internet.
questions.explanation: DNS is a service that translates domain names into IP addresses.

questions.question: What does URL refer to?
answers: The web address used to locate a page or resource online.
The transfer of data from the internet to a local device.
The transfer of data from a local device to the internet.
The amount of data that can move through a connection in a given time.
questions.explanation: URL is the web address used to locate a page or resource online.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Browser tab?
answers: A separate page open within the same browser window.
The transfer of data from a local device to the internet.
The amount of data that can move through a connection in a given time.
The delay before data begins moving across a network.
questions.explanation: Browser tab is a separate page open within the same browser window.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Download?
answers: The transfer of data from the internet to a local device.
The amount of data that can move through a connection in a given time.
The delay before data begins moving across a network.
Wireless networking that connects devices without cables.
questions.explanation: Download is the transfer of data from the internet to a local device.

questions.question: What does Upload refer to?
answers: The transfer of data from a local device to the internet.
The delay before data begins moving across a network.
Wireless networking that connects devices without cables.
A wired network connection commonly used for stable local access.
questions.explanation: Upload is the transfer of data from a local device to the internet.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Bandwidth?
answers: The amount of data that can move through a connection in a given time.
Wireless networking that connects devices without cables.
A wired network connection commonly used for stable local access.
A local area network that covers a small physical area.
questions.explanation: Bandwidth is the amount of data that can move through a connection in a given time.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Latency?
answers: The delay before data begins moving across a network.
A wired network connection commonly used for stable local access.
A local area network that covers a small physical area.
A wide area network that connects locations over large distances.
questions.explanation: Latency is the delay before data begins moving across a network.

questions.question: What does Wi-Fi refer to?
answers: Wireless networking that connects devices without cables.
A local area network that covers a small physical area.
A wide area network that connects locations over large distances.
A device or feature that shares internet access wirelessly.
questions.explanation: Wi-Fi is wireless networking that connects devices without cables.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Ethernet?
answers: A wired network connection commonly used for stable local access.
A wide area network that connects locations over large distances.
A device or feature that shares internet access wirelessly.
A company that provides internet access to customers.
questions.explanation: Ethernet is a wired network connection commonly used for stable local access.

questions.question: Which statement best describes LAN?
answers: A local area network that covers a small physical area.
A device or feature that shares internet access wirelessly.
A company that provides internet access to customers.
A small unit of data sent across a network.
questions.explanation: LAN is a local area network that covers a small physical area.

questions.question: What does WAN refer to?
answers: A wide area network that connects locations over large distances.
A company that provides internet access to customers.
A small unit of data sent across a network.
A web protocol used to transfer pages without encryption by default.
questions.explanation: WAN is a wide area network that connects locations over large distances.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Hotspot?
answers: A device or feature that shares internet access wirelessly.
A small unit of data sent across a network.
A web protocol used to transfer pages without encryption by default.
A secure web protocol that adds encryption to HTTP traffic.
questions.explanation: Hotspot is a device or feature that shares internet access wirelessly.

questions.question: Which statement best describes ISP?
answers: A company that provides internet access to customers.
A web protocol used to transfer pages without encryption by default.
A secure web protocol that adds encryption to HTTP traffic.
A human-readable website name such as example.com.
questions.explanation: ISP is a company that provides internet access to customers.

questions.question: What does Packet refer to?
answers: A small unit of data sent across a network.
A secure web protocol that adds encryption to HTTP traffic.
A human-readable website name such as example.com.
The main starting page of a website or browser.
questions.explanation: Packet is a small unit of data sent across a network.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is HTTP?
answers: A web protocol used to transfer pages without encryption by default.
A human-readable website name such as example.com.
The main starting page of a website or browser.
A service used to find information on the web.
questions.explanation: HTTP is a web protocol used to transfer pages without encryption by default.

questions.question: Which statement best describes HTTPS?
answers: A secure web protocol that adds encryption to HTTP traffic.
The main starting page of a website or browser.
A service used to find information on the web.
Small website data stored in a browser to remember settings or sessions.
questions.explanation: HTTPS is a secure web protocol that adds encryption to HTTP traffic.

questions.question: What does Domain name refer to?
answers: A human-readable website name such as example.com.
A service used to find information on the web.
Small website data stored in a browser to remember settings or sessions.
Temporary stored data that helps content load faster later.
questions.explanation: Domain name is a human-readable website name such as example.com.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Homepage?
answers: The main starting page of a website or browser.
Small website data stored in a browser to remember settings or sessions.
Temporary stored data that helps content load faster later.
A service that encrypts traffic and routes it through another network path.
questions.explanation: Homepage is the main starting page of a website or browser.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Search engine?
answers: A service used to find information on the web.
Temporary stored data that helps content load faster later.
A service that encrypts traffic and routes it through another network path.
A short-range wireless technology for connecting nearby devices.
questions.explanation: Search engine is a service used to find information on the web.

questions.question: What does Cookie refer to?
answers: Small website data stored in a browser to remember settings or sessions.
A service that encrypts traffic and routes it through another network path.
A short-range wireless technology for connecting nearby devices.
A scannable square code that quickly opens information or links.
questions.explanation: Cookie is small website data stored in a browser to remember settings or sessions.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Cache?
answers: Temporary stored data that helps content load faster later.
A short-range wireless technology for connecting nearby devices.
A scannable square code that quickly opens information or links.
Playing audio or video over the internet without downloading the full file first.
questions.explanation: Cache is temporary stored data that helps content load faster later.

questions.question: Which statement best describes VPN?
answers: A service that encrypts traffic and routes it through another network path.
A scannable square code that quickly opens information or links.
Playing audio or video over the internet without downloading the full file first.
An online service delivered over the internet instead of from only local software.
questions.explanation: VPN is a service that encrypts traffic and routes it through another network path.

questions.question: What does Bluetooth refer to?
answers: A short-range wireless technology for connecting nearby devices.
Playing audio or video over the internet without downloading the full file first.
An online service delivered over the internet instead of from only local software.
A live internet conversation that includes audio and video.
questions.explanation: Bluetooth is a short-range wireless technology for connecting nearby devices.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is QR code?
answers: A scannable square code that quickly opens information or links.
An online service delivered over the internet instead of from only local software.
A live internet conversation that includes audio and video.
A numeric label used to identify a device on a network.
questions.explanation: QR code is a scannable square code that quickly opens information or links.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Streaming?
answers: Playing audio or video over the internet without downloading the full file first.
A live internet conversation that includes audio and video.
A numeric label used to identify a device on a network.
A device that directs network traffic between devices and networks.
questions.explanation: Streaming is playing audio or video over the internet without downloading the full file first.

questions.question: What does Cloud service refer to?
answers: An online service delivered over the internet instead of from only local software.
A numeric label used to identify a device on a network.
A device that directs network traffic between devices and networks.
A device that connects a home or office network to an internet provider.
questions.explanation: Cloud service is an online service delivered over the internet instead of from only local software.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Video call?
answers: A live internet conversation that includes audio and video.
A device that directs network traffic between devices and networks.
A device that connects a home or office network to an internet provider.
A service that translates domain names into IP addresses.
questions.explanation: Video call is a live internet conversation that includes audio and video.
